module.name: require-bdd-spec
module.description: ESLint rule that enforces the presence and semantic validity of sibling .ts.bdd.json BDD specification files for every TypeScript source file.

scenarios.name: should skip files ending with .test.ts
scenarios.given: a TypeScript file whose name ends with .test.ts
scenarios.when: the rule's Program visitor is invoked
scenarios.then: no ESLint report is made regardless of BDD spec presence

scenarios.name: should report missingBddSpec when no sibling .ts.bdd.json file exists
scenarios.given: a non-test TypeScript source file with no sibling .ts.bdd.json
scenarios.when: the rule's Program visitor is invoked
scenarios.then: a missingBddSpec violation is reported with the expected spec path

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when the BDD spec file contains malformed JSON
scenarios.given: a non-test TypeScript source file with a sibling .ts.bdd.json that is not valid JSON
scenarios.when: the rule's Program visitor is invoked
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation is reported containing the parse error message

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when required top-level fields are absent
scenarios.given: a BDD spec file missing one or more of $schema, schemaVersion, sourceFile, module, specifications
scenarios.when: the rule validates the parsed spec object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation lists all missing required field names

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when $schema is not a string
scenarios.given: a BDD spec file where $schema is not a string value
scenarios.when: the rule validates the parsed spec object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports the type mismatch for $schema

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when schemaVersion does not equal 1.0.0
scenarios.given: a BDD spec file where schemaVersion is not exactly the string "1.0.0"
scenarios.when: the rule validates the parsed spec object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports the incorrect schemaVersion value

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when sourceFile is not a string
scenarios.given: a BDD spec file where sourceFile is not a string value
scenarios.when: the rule validates the parsed spec object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports the type mismatch for sourceFile

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when module is not a plain object
scenarios.given: a BDD spec file where module is an array or primitive
scenarios.when: the rule validates the parsed spec object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports that module must be an object

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when specifications is not an array
scenarios.given: a BDD spec file where specifications is a non-array value
scenarios.when: the rule validates the parsed spec object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports that specifications must be an array

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when sourceFile points to a non-existent file
scenarios.given: a BDD spec where sourceFile contains a path to a file that does not exist on disk
scenarios.when: the rule validates the sourceFile field
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports the non-existent path

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when module.name is empty or not a string
scenarios.given: a BDD spec where module.name is absent, empty, or not a string
scenarios.when: the rule validates the module object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports that module.name must be a non-empty string

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when module.description is empty or not a string
scenarios.given: a BDD spec where module.description is absent, empty, or not a string
scenarios.when: the rule validates the module object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports that module.description must be a non-empty string

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when module.exports is not an array
scenarios.given: a BDD spec where module.exports is a string, number, or object instead of an array
scenarios.when: the rule validates the module object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports that module.exports must be an array

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when a module.exports entry is not a non-empty string
scenarios.given: a BDD spec where module.exports contains a non-string or empty-string entry
scenarios.when: the rule validates the module object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation identifies the offending index

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when specifications array is empty
scenarios.given: a BDD spec with a specifications array that has zero entries
scenarios.when: the rule validates the specifications field
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports that specifications must not be empty

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when a feature entry is not an object
scenarios.given: a BDD spec where a specifications entry is null, a string, or a primitive
scenarios.when: the rule validates each feature
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation identifies the invalid feature entry

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when a feature is missing the feature name
scenarios.given: a BDD spec where a feature object has no "feature" string property
scenarios.when: the rule validates the feature object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports the missing feature name

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when a feature has an empty scenarios array
scenarios.given: a BDD spec where a feature's scenarios array is present but empty
scenarios.when: the rule validates the feature object
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports that scenarios must not be empty

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when a scenario is missing a required field
scenarios.given: a BDD spec where a scenario object lacks name, given, when, or then
scenarios.when: the rule validates each scenario
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation identifies the missing field and its location

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when a scenario name does not start with should
scenarios.given: a BDD spec where a scenario's name property does not begin with the word "should"
scenarios.when: the rule validates the scenario name
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation reports the non-conforming scenario name

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when module.exports lists a name not exported by the source
scenarios.given: a BDD spec whose module.exports contains an identifier that is not a named export of the companion TypeScript source
scenarios.when: the rule compares the declared exports to the actual source exports
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation names the undeclared export

scenarios.name: should report invalidBddSpec when the source exports a name absent from module.exports
scenarios.given: a TypeScript source file that exports an identifier not listed in the BDD spec's module.exports
scenarios.when: the rule compares the declared exports to the actual source exports
scenarios.then: an invalidBddSpec violation names the missing export

scenarios.name: should pass when module.exports exactly matches all named exports of the source
scenarios.given: a BDD spec whose module.exports is exactly the set of named exports in the TypeScript source
scenarios.when: the rule compares the declared exports to the actual source exports
scenarios.then: no violation is reported

scenarios.name: should aggregate all validation errors into a single invalidBddSpec report
scenarios.given: a BDD spec file that has multiple distinct validation failures
scenarios.when: the rule validates the spec
scenarios.then: a single invalidBddSpec violation is reported containing all error messages joined by newlines
